text: This is a first paper that classifies new data into predefined classes by the mean of already classified training data; in this paper, a support vector machine (SVM) is defined and implemented on a quantum computer. SVM is an optimized version of a binary classifier, and this quantum implementation gives logarithmic complexity vs vector length and the number of training samples, a clear exponential speed up over classical sampling algorithms that might need a polynomial time for big data. The paper contains a quantum big data algorithm which relies on nonsparse matrix exponentiation of a small matrix which contains the inverse of the training data inner-product (kernel) matrix. The matrix inversion is done efficiently, that is by linear complexity.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
